nameMeanings.name: Bailey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name for a steward or official, from Middle English bailli ‘manager, administrator’ (Old French baillis, from Late Latin baiulivus, an adjectival derivative of baiulus ‘attendant, carrier, porter’).
English: habitational name from Bailey in Little Mitton, Lancashire, named with Old English beg ‘berry’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

English: occasionally a topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, from Middle English (Old French) bailli ‘outer courtyard of a castle’ (Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’, a derivative of bailer ‘to enclose’). This term became a placename in its own right, denoting a district beside a fortification or wall, as in the case of the Old Bailey in London, which formed part of the early medieval outer wall of the city.

variants.name: Bailly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Marcel, Philippe.
French: occupational name for a steward or official, from Old French baillif. Compare Bailey 4.
French: habitational name from (Le) Bailly, the name of several places in the northern part of France.

variants.name: Bale
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, Middle English bailli, baile, from Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’ (see Bailey 3).
Breton (Balé): variant of Balay .
French: unexplained.

variants.name: Baily
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayly
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Balay
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Rajesh, Satish.
Altered form of English   Bailey  .
French: habitational name from any of various minor places so named.

variants.name: Baile
meanings: Spanish: status name for a steward or official, from Old Spanish baile, Late Latin baiulivus; cognate with English Bailey .
French (southern): variant of   Bail  .
English: variant of   Bale  .

variants.name: Baillie
meanings: Scottish:

from Older Scots baillie, baili. In Scotland, a baillie was a status name for any of various officials, including the chief magistrate in a barony and (later) the chief magistrate or administrative officer of a barony or district. Etymologically, this is the same word as English Bailey , but the functions as well as the spelling of the two officials were different in England and Scotland.
topographic name from Older Scots bailli ‘outer court of a castle’; see Bailey 3 and Bale 1.

variants.name: Baley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Bailey was born about 1445, in Brockton, Shropshire, England, her father, Walter Bailey, was 26 and her mother, Matiolda Matilda Hamerton, was 21. She had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with William Hayward. She died in November 1489, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 45, and was buried in Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Bailey est née environ 1445, à Brockton, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter Bailey, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Matiolda Matilda Hamerton, avait 21 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec William Hayward. Elle est décédée en novembre 1489, à Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Bailey um 1445 in Brockton, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter Bailey 26 und ihre Mutter Matiolda Matilda Hamerton 21. Sie hatte mit William Hayward mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb im November 1489 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Shropshire, England und wurde in Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Bailey è nata il circa 1445 nel Brockton, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Walter Bailey, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Matiolda Matilda Hamerton, aveva 21 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con William Hayward. Morì nel novembre 1489, nel Shropshire, England, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolta a Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Baileyが約1445年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアブロクトンで生まれた時，父Walter Baileyは26歳，母Matiolda Matilda Hamertonは21歳であった。彼女はWilliam Haywardとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1489年11月にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで45歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Bailey(이)가 대략 1445년 영국 슈럽실르 브록턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter Bailey(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matiolda Matilda Hamerton(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 William Hayward(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1489년 11월 영국 슈럽실르에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Bailey nasceu aproximadamente 1445, em Brockton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter Bailey, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Matiolda Matilda Hamerton, tinha 21 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas com William Hayward. Ela faleceu em novembro de 1489, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 45 anos, e foi sepultada em Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Bailey родилась около 1445 г. в местности брокктон, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter Bailey было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Matiolda Matilda Hamerton, 21. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от William Hayward. Она умерла ноябрь 1489 г. в местности шропшир, Англия в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Bailey nació aproximadamente 1445, en Brockton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter Bailey, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Matiolda Matilda Hamerton, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas con William Hayward. Murió en noviembre de 1489, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultada en Shropshire, Inglaterra.
